name: White Sauce Mix
recipeInstructions:
1. This batch makes about 12 one-cup servings. Each serving costs approximately 16 cents.
2. The entire batch fits snugly in a 3-pound empty shortening can.
3. Store the mix in the refrigerator as it is used less frequently than some other recipes due to the inclusion of margarine.
4. Label and wrap the recipe securely on the can using clear contact paper for easy wiping.
5. For each one-cup serving of white sauce:
- Use 1/2 cup White Sauce Mix
- Add 1 cup of cool water
- Heat until boiling
6. If using natural flavorings such as beef, chicken, or turkey drippings (including fat):
- Place the drippings in a container and freeze until the fat rises to the top and hardens.
- Remove from the freezer and discard the floating fat.
- Optionally, add an ice cube to speed up the hardening process.
7. Add some of the hardened drippings (no more than 1 tablespoon at a time) to the white sauce to achieve the desired flavor.
- If no natural drippings are available, use 1/2 teaspoon of chicken or beef soup base per serving.
- Be cautious as these may be high in salt.
- For a richer, darker gravy, add 1 teaspoon of kitchen bouquet.
8. To prepare potato soup using the white sauce mix:
- Follow the instructions for making white sauce and incorporate into your preferred potato soup recipe from the Frozen Assets List.
